mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2020-12-01 indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500 thousand persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 500 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142,624 thousand as of 2020-12-01 reflects post-shock recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with strong labor rebound
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window following trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) below trend signals demand contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage revision from 18596.521 to 20558.879 indicates significant data uncertainty
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict drawdowns in recession periods (NBER-dated contractions).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating below 2% month-over-month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage revision from 105.7244 to 98.363 indicates measurement uncertainty
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 52% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1.5 million annualized units signals residential demand strength that accelerates construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average exceeds 1.5 million units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,547 thousand units as of 2020-11-01 reflects post-pandemic housing surge
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict returns during housing downturns (HOUST < 1.0 million annualized).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.7% as of 2020-12-01 reflects pandemic labor market damage
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 54% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict equity weakness in NBER-dated recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerating above 5% year-over-year combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE falling > 0.2pp over 3 months) signals robust consumer demand and labor market tightness that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth > 5% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 540,915 million dollars as of 2020-12-01 reflects holiday demand strength
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 62% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term fails to show Rank IC > 0.04 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3.0% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0% signals monetary policy lag that creates real rate compression, but if sustained beyond 6 months triggers expectations of future tightening that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0% sustained for 6+ months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 261.775 (vintage) as of 2020-12-01 shows low inflation environment
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation acceleration and tightening expectations
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 53% of sustained joint-trigger instances, or the mechanism fails to predict equity weakness in periods of actual Fed tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising > 1.5pp over 12 months).
